name: John Kirby
abstract: John Kirby (December 31, 1908 – June 14, 1952), was an American jazz double-bassist and bandleader. In addition to sideman work (prominently with Benny Goodman), Kirby lead a chamber jazz sextet in the late 1930s and early 1940s, which scored several hit songs including "Loch Lomond" and the debut recording of "Undecided", a jazz standar…

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: I played with John Kirby's band. Took Buster Bailey's place for a few months.

about_quotes.speaker: Russell Procope
about_quotes.evidence: I went straight from Teddy Hill to John Kirby... Kirby was looking for someone to play Maxine's music... my entrance into the band you might say was to play Maxine's music.

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Moore
about_quotes.evidence: Myself, Pete Brown, Don Frye, of course John Kirby... you left Kirby... I proved myself inside of two years... John Kirby, he wasn't a bandleader at all.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: I went with Kirby's band in 1947... In the band there was Buster Bailey, Charlie Shavers and myself in the front line, and there was John Kirby

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Jerome
about_quotes.evidence: at night I used to sit in with John Kirby’s six pieces on 52nd Street... And I played not tenor, but bass clarinet with John Kirby.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Jerome interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: He quit drinking and he joined George, uh, John Kirby. He hadn't had a drink in 6 months and I went in Joe Hawkes place
